title: Someone broke the Code of Conduct

intro.description: We respond with accountability and repair — the goal is to keep the space safe AND help a young person grow, not to push them out.

q0.text: Is it serious — threats, doxxing, grooming, sexual content involving a minor, anything illegal?

s1.headline: De-escalate in the moment.
steps: Tell them to chill, use a short timeout, !breathe / !chill.
Keep it light where you can — the goal is a reset, not a scene.

s2.headline: Address it so the space stays visibly safe.
steps: Delete-with-dignity: say why, then remove — or ask them to. Reply, redirect.
Or empower them to address it themselves — :villager: reaction, MEE6 commands, targeted validation of others, humour if appropriate.

q2.text: Did the incident involve more than one person?

answers.label: Yes — it’s a conflict between people

hint.label: Why does this matter?
hint.body: @Hush is the response ladder for repeat image misuse. Repeat non-image behaviour gets a belonging response instead — sometimes rapport does the work a mute can’t.

q4.text: Has it happened more than twice in the last three months?

q5.text: Has it happened more than twice that you’re aware of?

q6.text: Have they had the @Hush role before?
q6.roleNote: The @Hush ladder is a Facilitator action. If you’re a Moderator: post the pattern in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators.

q7.text: What’s the longest they’ve had it?
q7.roleNote: The @Hush ladder is a Facilitator action. If you’re a Moderator: post the pattern in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators.

s3.headline: Post it, then wait at least 12 hours.
steps: Post it in #mod-chat or #flags-log.
Then give it time — people often sort it out themselves. Give them the chance.

q8.text: Have the people involved reached resolution on their own?

s4.headline: The @Facilitator with the most rapport DMs to find out what they need.
steps: Not a lecture — a check-in. What’s going on for them, and what would help?
s4.roleNote: This is a Facilitator action. If you’re a Moderator: post it in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators.

q9.text: Is there a pattern of conflict in this relationship?

q10.text: Are they willing to get support disrupting the relational pattern in a group chat or call together?

q11.text: Are they willing to get support getting to a solution?

q12.text: Are they willing to get support with self-regulation?

end-redirect.headline: This ladder is for everyday stuff — that’s a critical incident.
steps: Go to the Critical Incident tool now.

end-log.headline: Post it in #mod-chat or #flags-log and move on.
steps: You handled it.

end-house.headline: Ask their House leader to ping them in their House channel to build rapport.
steps: Support doesn’t have to be formal — sometimes belonging does the work.
Then post it in #mod-chat or #flags-log.

end-hush-3.headline: Add @Hush for 3 days.
steps: The Facilitator with the most rapport DMs them about it — @Hush is a reset, not a punishment, with a path back via a Facilitator.
Then post in #mod-chat / update #hush-log.

moderator.headline: The @Hush ladder is a Facilitator action.
steps: Post the pattern in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators — include message links.
A Facilitator applies the role and DMs the member.

end-hush-7.headline: Add @Hush for 7 days.
steps: The Facilitator with the most rapport DMs them about it — @Hush is a reset, not a punishment, with a path back via a Facilitator.
Then post in #mod-chat / update #hush-log.

moderator.headline: The @Hush ladder is a Facilitator action.
steps: Post the pattern in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators — include message links.
A Facilitator applies the role and DMs the member.

end-hush-indef.headline: Add @Hush indefinitely.
steps: The Facilitator with the most rapport DMs them about it — @Hush is a reset, not a punishment, with a path back via a Facilitator.
Then post in #mod-chat / update #hush-log.

moderator.headline: The @Hush ladder is a Facilitator action.
steps: Post the pattern in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators — include message links.
A Facilitator applies the role and DMs the member.

end-resolved.headline: Update #mod-chat / #flags-log.
steps: Optionally: ask their House leader to ping them in their House channel and build some rapport.

end-transform.headline: Use conflict transformation techniques.
steps: Dialogue methods, circle process, transformative mediation.
Then log it in #mod-chat / #flags-log.

end-resolve.headline: Use conflict resolution techniques.
steps: Shuttle diplomacy, joint problem-solving, planning next steps.
Then log it in #mod-chat / #flags-log.

end-skills.headline: Use skill-building techniques.
steps: Self-awareness, mindfulness, communication.
Then log it in #mod-chat / #flags-log.

end-house-2.headline: Ask their House leader to ping them in their House channel to build rapport.
steps: Support doesn’t have to be formal — sometimes belonging does the work.
Then log it in #mod-chat / #flags-log.
